name: GF(2^8) finite-field arithmetic
task: Create /tmp/eval_gf256/gf.py with two functions over GF(2^8) using the AES irreducible polynomial 0x11b: gf256_mul(a, b, modulus=0x11b) — Russian-peasant style: shift-XOR loop with conditional reduction by modulus when the high bit overflows; gf256_inv(a, modulus=0x11b) — multiplicative inverse via Fermat's little theorem (a^254). Raise ValueError when a=0 in inv.

validation.script:
import importlib.util; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m','/tmp/eval_gf256/gf.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); M, I = mod.gf256_mul, mod.gf256_inv; assert M(0x57, 0x83) == 0xc1, 'FIPS-197 example'; assert M(1, 0xab) == 0xab
for a in [0x01, 0x57, 0x83, 0xff, 0xab]:
    assert M(a, I(a)) == 1, f'inv {hex(a)}'
try: I(0)
except ValueError: pass
else: raise AssertionError
print('OK')
